https://kmc.du.ac.in/kmcouter/collnews/3.title: APPLICATION FOR COLLEGE FEE CONCESSION FOR NEEDY STUDENTS
https://kmc.du.ac.in/kmcouter/collnews/3.text:
Application for College Fee Concession for Needy Students

Full or partial concessions of the college fees for the academic year 2025-26 may be available to students who are facing financial hardship. Such students, who meet the eligibility criterion below and are desirous of availing the fee concession, may apply to the college in prescribed e-form accessible at the Kirori Mal College website at the following link, along with the scanned copies of all relevant documents, latest by

January 23, 2026

:

https://forms.gle/5UTXRsKb3hK3bKFq6

Eligibility Criterion for Application:

For General Category (including EWS) Students:

Students, whose parents’/guardian’s combined gross annual income from all sources does not exceed

Rs.4,20,000 per annum

. A 10% relaxation of the income limit is applicable for the female students.

For SC/ST/OBC Category Students:

Students, whose parents’/guardian’s combined gross annual income from all sources does not exceed

Rs.4,80,000 per annum

. A 10% relaxation of the income limit is applicable for the female students.

Details about the scanned copies of the documents required to be submitted with the form are mentioned in the form itself.

NOTE:

There is only limited fund available for fee concession, so students who are not genuinely needy are requested not to apply for this.

The committee will call the applicants for a face to face interview on a notified date and may also ask for any additional information or documents.

The decision to allow fee concession and the extent of it for each individual applicant will be made by the Fee Concession Committee of the college. The committee’s decision will be final regarding this matter.

Any student found guilty of submitting false proof of income or making false claims will face strict disciplinary action.

30.12.2025                                                                            Saumyajit Bhattacharya

Convenor, Fee Concession Committee

Here’s a scholarship opportunity for candidates residing in Delhi pursuing the final year of their undergraduate courses in any stream at any government institute in Delhi!

BYPL SASHAKT Scholarship 2025-26

The BYPL SASHAKT Scholarship 2025-26, launched by BSES Yamuna Power Limited (BYPL), offers up to INR 30,000 to support the higher education of meritorious students from economically weaker sections. This initiative aims to empower students to complete their studies and achieve their aspirations.

Eligibility:

- Open to Indian nationals residing in Delhi

- Must be in the final year of an undergraduate course in any stream at a government institute in Delhi

- Students enrolled in distance learning courses are also eligible to apply.

- Applicants must have a minimum score of 55% marks in the last exam.

- Annual family income must not exceed INR 6,00,000.

Award: Scholarship of up to INR 30,000

Application Deadline: 21 November 2025

Application URL: http://www.b4s.in/a/ggl_BYPL6_20250929_1

Share this message with someone who needs this scholarship and help them to achieve their academic goals.

For any further queries, feel free to email byplsashakt@buddy4study.com and call at 011-430-92248 (Ext- 210)

https://kmc.du.ac.in/kmcouter/collnews/3.text:
GUIDELINES FOR PREVENTION OF DEFACEMENT OF PROPERTY

KIRORI MAL COLLEGE, UNIVERSITY OF DELHI

Date:  02.09.2025

College Committee for Prevention of Defacement of Property

NOTICE

As per the notification (DU/124/LEGAL-6272(17)/2025/237), issued by University of Delhi,

In order to avoid any defacement of the College property following guidelines are issued by The College Committee for Prevention of Defacement of Property.

Any act of Defacement/Damage to public property and nonadherence to these Guidelines shall amount to gross indiscipline. It shall attract consequent punishment provisions in the relevant Ordinance of the University.

Defacement of college property includes sticking of bills/posters/stickers on non- designated areas/spaces, writing on walls/furniture/trees etc. as well as any sort of alterations in the original state of the college property.

In case of nonadherence to the notified guidelines by indulging in any act of defacement, person(s) benefiting shall be liable for fines up to Rs 25,000.

Students/Societies/Departments are encouraged to use e-communication platforms (such as emails, messaging apps, and social media) for campaigning/promotion, thereby moving away from paper-based materials.

No bills shall be pasted at spaces in the college other than those designated by the committee.

The list of new designated spaces/Walls of democracy is as follows :

Towards Hostel Entry Gate (For College)

Outside Library (For College)

Backside of Academic Auditorium towards Canteen Lawn (For College)

Towards the entry to Main building (For DUSU candidates)

Principal

Kirori Mal College

https://kmc.du.ac.in/kmcouter/collnews/2.text:
Link -

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScWcGxGltR-Z9VlQOpV9WykKttr5ByKPWRuP6JrpHXADjcyBw/viewform?usp=preview

In a landmark step toward Democratic-Civic engagement and participatory governance, Kirori Mal College proudly announces the formal declaration of the MLA Internship Programme. This initiative brings together the Office of the MLA (Model Town Constituency) & Kirori Mal College.

A visionary initiative led by our respected Principal, Prof. Dinesh Khattar, this initiative opens new doors for KMC students to learn beyond classroom and textbooks learning - to directly engage with day to day basis democratic legislative processes, civic structures, citizen participation, governance models and direct access to constituency.

What this means for our students:

• Internships in constituency research, community outreach, and public policy

• Mentorship from the Office of the MLA

• Training and field support by Neecop Consultants

• Academic guidance and coordination from KMC faculty

• Joint certification upon completion

This is not just an internship-it's an opportunity to become a part of the day to day democratic civic process, contribute meaningfully to society, and explore the workings of government firsthand.

An education that moves beyond the classroom-into the heart of democracy.

Dear Students,

We are pleased to announce the launch of our

ERP portal

for the collection of data in

Generic Electives Courses (GEC) and Ability Enhancement Courses (AEC) for Semester 1.

This dynamic portal aims to streamline the process of course allocation and ensure efficient data management. All students are required to complete and submit their Course Allocation Form (CAF) through the ERP portal. Please note that if the CAF is not filled out and submitted by the deadline, the college will, at its discretion, allot the courses to you.

Important Details:

-Deadline for CAF Submission:

11:59 PM

, Sunday 17

th

August 2025

erp@kmc.du.ac.in

Please read the detailed notices on the college website to help you make the right choices before you decide on course selection and filling out the CAF. Two separate notices exist for (i) Semester I – all Honors and B.Sc. (Prog.) students, and (ii) Semester I – B.Com. and B.A (Prog.) student.

Students are urged to adhere to the deadline and ensure their forms are accurately filled out and submitted on time.

Thank you

Best wishes,

Prof. Dinesh Khattar

Principal

4.

You need to submit three preferences for GEC in order of priority. The three choices must be different, meaning the same course cannot be listed more than once. These options are available in the attached list. While we aim to assign the student’s first choice, we may not always succeed because of the high volume of applicants for each course. Therefore, please carefully select your second and third preferences.

5.

.

GECs of a discipline are offered to the students who are not studying the discipline as one of their core courses (DSCs), as it is meant to provide

multidisciplinary perspectives

. So, you

cannot

opt for GECs offered by your parent/core department(s).

7.

Ability Enhancement Courses (AECs):

(i)

All students pursuing a B.Sc. (Hons) or B.Sc. (Prog.) must select an AEC from a pool of Indian Languages. They are required to enroll in courses at different levels based on their previous proficiency in the language, such as whether they have studied it through Class XII, Class X, Class VIII, or not at all.

The college offers AECs in four languages:

Hindi, Sanskrit, Urdu,

and

Bengali

. Additionally, students may choose to study any other Indian language offered centrally by the language departments at the university or through any other college participating under the North Cluster Teaching scheme. In such cases, students should indicate their preferred language.

Please note that once an Indian language is selected as an AEC in Semester 1, the same language must be continued in Semester 3. No changes in language or level are allowed in Semester 3.

(ii)

All students of BA(Hons) and B.Com(Hons) have to opt for Environmental Science (Course 1).

II.

on the ERP Portal for B.Com. and B.A. Prog. students of Semester-I 2025

All Semester I students of B.Com and BA (Prog.)

under

NEP-

UGCF

in

Kirori

Mal

College

, please note that they are required to submit their respective preferences for Generic Elective Courses (GEC-1) and Ability Enhancement Courses (AEC-1), and on the ERP Portal,

latest by

, Sunday 17

th

August 2025

.

.

Please note that the choices have to be made on the ERP Portal

ONLY.

1.

Log in to your ERP portal. You will see an option called COURSE SELECTION. After selecting that, select your semester. Next, Click to Get the Form.

2.

Please carefully read

this

Notice

and the instructions mentioned in the portal (at the beginning of various categories of selections)

before

filling

up

your preferences.

3.

The form can be edited as many times till the set deadline (

11:59 PM

, Sunday 17

th

August 2025

).

However, after this deadline, the form will be closed and no further editing will be possible.

4.

BA Programme students must take the English GEC this semester, except for those whose main subject is the English Discipline. Students with English Discipline as their core subject will need to choose from the Common Pool GECs, excluding courses from their two main disciplines. This is because Generic Elective Courses (GECs) for a discipline are meant for students not studying that discipline as a core subject. Therefore, they cannot select GECs offered by their own department, and such options will not be available. For example, BA (prog.) students with English and Political Science as their core disciplines cannot pick English or Political Science GECs from the common pool. All students choosing a GEC from the common pool must submit three preferred courses, listed in order.

5.

All B.Com (prog.) students, except for those mentioned in the next point, must take Hindi GEC this semester. The level at which they take Hindi GEC will depend on their previous Hindi studies in school. Please note that B.Com (prog.) students cannot change the level (A, B, or C) in Semester III from what they studied in Semester I.

6.

However, all such students of B.Com. Programme and those who have not studied Hindi up to Class VIII or beyond or have not studied it at all in school (including all foreign students), are to choose their GEC from the common pool. However, while selecting their GEC from the common pool, they cannot choose the Commerce GEC because their parent department offers it. All such students opting for a GEC from the common pool have to provide three choices of GE courses in order of preference.

7.

No request for a change of courses will be entertained once the choice for the selection of courses has been exercised

.

8.

All students of BA(Prog) and B.Com(Prog) have to opt for Environmental Science (Course 1).

provide your second and third preferences with due consideration

.

high

demand

".

For the purpose of verification, the candidates are required to bring all their original certificates and testimonials regarding academic qualification, reserved category, work experience, and research experience. The candidates are also required to bring their original documents/certificates/

testimonials/evidence of indexing/citations regarding the details mentioned in their online application along with a valid photo-Id document (Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Passport/Voter ID Card/Driving License).

The candidates are also required to submit an additional set of their application form, self-attested photocopy of the testimonials, certificates, publications, experience letters,

reserved category certificate

and other relevant documents at the time of presentation.

The onus for proving the authenticity of the relevant documents/certificates/

testimonials uploaded with the online application will be that of the candidate, although the College reserves the right to adopt the mechanism it deems fit for ascertaining the authenticity of these documents/certificates/

testimonials.

The candidates are also expected to place their M.Phil. dissertation and Ph.D. thesis, wherever applicable, and best and latest publications, in original, before the Presentation Assessment Committee during the presentation.

The candidates serving in Government/Public Sector Undertakings (including Board/Autonomous Bodies) are required to submit ‘No Objection Certificate’ from their employer at the time of interview. The NOC should also indicate the vigilance and integrity certificate from the parent department.

As indicated, this is only a communication informing about the schedule of First Stage and Second Stage of the selection process for the posts detailed above. The appearance in the presentation (First Stage) will be subject to the verification of the documents/information/

testimonials submitted by the candidates.

Appearing for the presentation (First Stage) does not guarantee selection/appointment. No representation will be entertained in this respect.

The college is not responsible for any boarding/lodging expenses and T.A./D.A. of the candidates.

The candidates are advised to check the college website regularly for further updates.

Emails to all the shortlisted candidates have also been sent regarding the above schedule by official college e-mail

‘info

@kmc.du.ac.in

’.

In case of any exigency, College reserves the right to cancel or postpone the presentation without assigning any reason thereof.

Note

: The lists of the provisionally shortlisted candidates annexed along with this letter contain the details of the shortlisted candidates of respective categories in the ascending order of their application number.

PRINCIPAL
